name: computer-vision-engineering
description: Computer-vision engineering team — 3 agents (cv-systems-architect, cv-model-engineer, vision-deployment-engineer) for the three engines of a production vision build: system architecture (task framing across classification / detection / segmentation / OCR / pose / tracking / VLM, data & annotation strategy, CNN-vs-ViT-vs-foundation-model choice, build-vs-fine-tune-vs-API, deployment-target selection across cloud GPU / edge / embedded / browser, and eval-metric design — mAP / IoU / precision-recall at the operating point), model engineering (dataset curation & augmentation, transfer learning / fine-tuning, model selection across YOLO / DETR / SAM / CLIP / EfficientNet / ViT, loss & metric design, active learning / hard-negative mining, class imbalance, the evaluation harness, drift/regression detection), and inference optimization & deployment (quantization / pruning / distillation, export/runtime across ONNX / TensorRT / CoreML / TFLite / OpenVINO, edge/embedded targets — Jetson / mobile NPU / Coral, batching & throughput, streaming-video pipelines with frame sampling / ROI / tracking-by-detection, latency budgets, camera integration). Ships 4 skills, a decision-tree knowledge bank (4 Mermaid trees + a dated 2026 reference), 5 best-practices, 2 templates, 2 commands. Engineering judgment, not a benchmark leaderboard or a compliance verdict; the model/hardware/runtime/annotation-tool landscape is volatile — every model name, version, accelerator spec, and accuracy/latency number carries a retrieval date + [verify-at-use]; no PII, no image data stored. Requires ravenclaude-core@>=0.7.0.
